name: Ensure AMIs Are Private
body:
### Security intent

This control reduces unnecessary exposure by ensuring that **aMIs Are Private**.

### Network condition reviewed

The assessment reviews aMIs that are public in AWS. It focuses on rules, endpoints, listeners, or policies that make the service reachable in a riskier way than intended.

### Expected posture

Public reachability should be deliberate, documented, and tightly scoped. Administrative and data-plane paths should use private connectivity, strong TLS, explicit source ranges, and provider-native controls rather than broad internet access.
remediationDescription:
## AWS remediation

### Console
1. Open the AMI in EC2.
2. Select Actions -> Edit AMI permissions.
3. Remove public launch permission (All).
4. Keep sharing only with specific AWS accounts if needed.

### AWS CLI
~~~bash
aws ec2 modify-image-attribute \
  --image-id <ami-id> \
  --launch-permission "Remove=[{Group=all}]"

aws ec2 modify-image-attribute \
  --image-id <ami-id> \
  --launch-permission "Add=[{UserId=<account-id>}]"
~~~

### References
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/sharingamis-explicit.html
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/ec2/modify-image-attribute.html
